ace_statements.source_text: A form of identification issued consistent with the requirements of the REAL ID Act of 2005 that indicates the applicant is a citizen of the United States.
ace_statements.ace_text: If a person has a REAL-ID-form-of-identification and the REAL-ID-form-of-identification indicates that the person is a citizen-of-the-United-States then the person has documentary-proof-of-citizenship.
ace_statements.notes: APE not available in current environment.

ace_statements.ace_text: If a person has a valid-United-States-passport then the person has documentary-proof-of-citizenship.

ace_statements.source_text: The applicant's official United States military identification card, together with a United States military record of service showing that the applicant's place of birth was in the United States.
ace_statements.ace_text: If a person has a United-States-military-identification-card and the person has a military-record-of-service and the military-record-of-service shows that the place-of-birth of the person is in the United-States then the person has documentary-proof-of-citizenship.

ace_statements.source_text: A valid government-issued photo identification card issued by a Federal, State or Tribal government showing that the applicant's place of birth was in the United States.
ace_statements.ace_text: If a person has a government-issued-photo-identification-card and the government-issued-photo-identification-card shows that the place-of-birth of the person is in the United-States then the person has documentary-proof-of-citizenship.

ace_statements.source_text: A valid government-issued photo identification card... but only if presented together with one or more of the following: (A) A certified birth certificate... (E) A Naturalization Certificate... (F) An American Indian Card...
ace_statements.ace_text: If a person has a government-issued-photo-identification-card and the person has a certified-birth-certificate then the person has documentary-proof-of-citizenship. If a person has a government-issued-photo-identification-card and the person has a naturalization-certificate then the person has documentary-proof-of-citizenship. If a person has a government-issued-photo-identification-card and the person has an American-Indian-Card-KIC then the person has documentary-proof-of-citizenship.
ace_statements.notes: Compound disjunction split into separate ACE statements. Certified birth certificate has seven sub-requirements (§ 3(b)(5)(A)(i)-(vii)) not fully decomposed here.

ace_statements.source_text: Under any method of voter registration in a State, the State shall not accept and process an application to register to vote in an election for Federal office unless the applicant presents documentary proof of United States citizenship with the application.
ace_statements.ace_text: If a person submits a voter-registration-application for a federal-election and the person does not present documentary-proof-of-citizenship with the voter-registration-application then the State does not accept the voter-registration-application.
ace_statements.notes: Core operative prohibition. 'Any method' includes motor-voter, mail, agency-based, and online registration.

ace_statements.source_text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this Act, a State may not register an individual to vote in elections for Federal office held in the State unless, at the time the individual applies to register to vote, the individual provides documentary proof of United States citizenship.
ace_statements.ace_text: If a person applies to register-to-vote in a federal-election and the person does not provide documentary-proof-of-citizenship at the time-of-application then the State does not register the person.
ace_statements.notes: Restates and strengthens the § 4(b) prohibition with 'notwithstanding' clause.

ace_statements.source_text: each State shall establish a process under which an applicant who cannot provide documentary proof of United States citizenship... may, if the applicant signs an attestation under penalty of perjury... submit such other evidence... and such official shall make a determination as to whether the applicant has sufficiently established United States citizenship
ace_statements.ace_text: If a person cannot provide documentary-proof-of-citizenship and the person signs an attestation-under-perjury that the person is a citizen-of-the-United-States then the person may submit other-evidence to a State-or-local-official. If a State-or-local-official receives other-evidence from a person then the State-or-local-official determines whether the person has sufficiently-established-citizenship.
ace_statements.notes: CRITICAL AMBIGUITY: 'sufficiently established' is undefined. Whether this process guarantees registration or is discretionary is a key interpretive hinge.

ace_statements.source_text: A State shall remove an individual who is not a citizen of the United States from the official list of eligible voters for elections for Federal office held in the State at any time upon receipt of documentation or verified information that a registrant is not a United States citizen.
ace_statements.ace_text: If a State receives documentation-or-verified-information that a registrant is not a citizen-of-the-United-States then the State removes the registrant from the official-list-of-eligible-voters.
ace_statements.notes: 'At any time' and 'verified information' raise due process questions about notice and opportunity to be heard before removal.

ace_statements.source_text: in the case of an officer or employee of the executive branch, providing material assistance to a noncitizen in attempting to register to vote or vote in an election for Federal office
ace_statements.ace_text: If an officer-or-employee of the executive-branch provides material-assistance to a noncitizen and the noncitizen attempts to register-to-vote in a federal-election then the officer-or-employee commits a criminal-offense.
ace_statements.notes: 'Material assistance' is undefined. Could have chilling effect on election officials.

ace_statements.source_text: registering an applicant to vote in an election for Federal office who fails to present documentary proof of United States citizenship
ace_statements.ace_text: If an election-official registers a person to vote in a federal-election and the person does not present documentary-proof-of-citizenship then the election-official commits a criminal-offense.

ace_statements.source_text: Nothing in this Act... may be construed to supercede, restrict, or otherwise affect the ability of an individual to cast a provisional ballot in an election for Federal office or to have the ballot counted in the election if the individual is verified as a citizen
ace_statements.ace_text: If a person casts a provisional-ballot in a federal-election and the person is later verified as a citizen-of-the-United-States then the provisional-ballot is counted.

ace_statements.source_text: An applicant who submits the mail voter registration application form... shall not be registered to vote in an election for Federal office unless (A) the applicant presents documentary proof of United States citizenship in person to the office of the appropriate election official not later than the deadline...
ace_statements.ace_text: If a person submits a mail-voter-registration-application and the person does not present documentary-proof-of-citizenship in-person to the election-official before the registration-deadline then the person is not registered to vote.
ace_statements.notes: In-person presentation requirement effectively eliminates fully remote mail registration for voters without REAL ID or passport.
